task_completion.description: Verify that the BlueMail application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The BlueMail application window and its interface elements (inbox, folders, etc.) are visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the search function in BlueMail has been accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar is visible, focused, or the search icon has been clicked within the BlueMail interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the correct search keyword was entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Sequences Window' is typed into the BlueMail search bar.

task_completion.description: Verify that the search results successfully located the target email(s).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search results list shows at least one email containing 'Sequences Window' (e.g., with the subject 'Re: New Sequences Window').

task_completion.description: Verify that the target email has been opened and is being viewed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The email containing 'Sequences Window' is actively open in the reading pane or a separate window, displaying its full subject ('Re: New Sequences Window') and body content.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the graphical user interface rather than terminal commands to find the email.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open showing commands like 'grep' or 'find' being used to search for the keyword in the mail directory.

integrity.description: Verify that the email is being viewed inside the BlueMail application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The active window is BlueMail, not a text editor (like gedit or nano) or a file manager viewing raw .eml files.

integrity.description: Verify that the email is rendered properly by an email client.
integrity.visual_evidence: The email is displayed with standard email client UI elements (e.g., Reply, Forward, formatted From/To headers) rather than as plain text source code with raw MIME headers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The exact search keyword is 'Sequences Window'. There are exactly 2 matching emails in the corpus, both with the subject 'Re: New Sequences Window'. The agent must open at least one of these specific emails within the BlueMail application.
